authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Landforms layer record for "Boggy Meadow" (gaz_id 1667631), Flat class, Fresno Co., CA (GNIS has no Stream-class record for "Boggy Creek" anywhere in California)
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":1667631,"gaz_name":"Boggy Meadow","gaz_featureclass":"Flat","fcode":15028,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Fresno"}, geometry (Web Mercator -13248703.8946, 4491888.4132) converts to lon -119.0151, lat 37.3777, in the upper Mono Creek drainage tributary to Lake Thomas A. Edison. GNIS's Streams (Mouth) layer has zero "Boggy Creek" records in California (confirmed by a nationwide query on that name — only FL/NE/OK/TX/AR/LA/GA/AZ/WA/ND/WY/SC/AL entries exist, none in CA), so this same-named Boggy Meadow landform — a Sierra Nevada meadow conventionally named for the creek draining it — is used as the best available GNIS anchor; the other two "Boggy Meadow" GNIS records (gaz_id 253422 and 219587) are in Madera Co. and unrelated. Corrects the file's prior coordinate (-118.9950, 37.3600), which sat ~2.6km southeast of this point and coincided almost exactly with the unrelated Mono Creek Campground's published coordinates (37.3585535, -118.9984518), suggesting it was guessed from a nearby landmark rather than pulled from GNIS.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(14) — Boggy Creek
sources.quotedText: (14) Boggy Creek (Fresno Co.) and tributaries (tributary to Thomas Edison Lake). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. 5 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(a) — General Provisions for waters listed in the special-regulations table
sources.quotedText: Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

regulations.humanSummary: Boggy Creek and tributaries open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through September 30
regulations.verbatimText: Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30.

parameters.counting_rule: The table entry sets a 5-trout daily bag limit; per 14 CCR 7.50(a)(4), unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit, so the possession limit also equals 5.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag and possession limit

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
